articles.headline: Teen arrested after bringing stolen gun to Nashville high school
articles.image_alt: Stratford High School in Nashville, Tennessee
articles.excerpt: A 17-year-old Stratford High School student was arrested April 29 after the campus Evolv weapon-detection system flagged a stolen handgun in his backpack at the entrance. He faces theft of a firearm and possession of a firearm on school property charges.

articles.headline: Hillsboro student taken into custody after being found with gun
articles.image_alt: Hillsboro High School in Jefferson County, Missouri
articles.excerpt: A Hillsboro High School student was taken into custody April 29 after staff found an unloaded handgun and ammunition on him during a search prompted by a tip. Jefferson County deputies took the student into custody without incident; classes continued.

articles.headline: Former student armed with gun sparks havoc at Central Florida high school
articles.image_alt: Central Florida high school exterior with police response
articles.excerpt: A former student carrying a firearm entered a Central Florida high school on April 29, prompting an emergency response and lockdown. Deputies detained the suspect on campus; no shots were fired and no students were injured before officers secured the scene.

articles.headline: Report of possible weapon on campus leads to lockdown at Halifax County High School
articles.image_alt: Halifax County High School in Virginia
articles.excerpt: Halifax County High School in Virginia was placed on lockdown April 28 after administrators were alerted to a possible weapon on campus. A search recovered a look-alike item rather than a functional firearm; the lockdown was lifted later that afternoon.

articles.headline: Sierra HS student arrested after allegedly threatening peer with loaded gun in school bathroom
articles.image_alt: Sierra High School in Colorado Springs
articles.excerpt: A Sierra High School student was arrested April 27 after a school resource officer responding to a bathroom disturbance found a loaded .380 handgun in the student's waistband. Police say the weapon was used to threaten another student but never discharged.

articles.headline: Three MLK High School students charged with battery after fighting teacher
articles.image_alt: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. High School entrance sign
articles.excerpt: Three students at Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. High School in DeKalb County, Georgia were charged with battery and disrupting public school after a hallway fight with a veteran teacher on April 22. Video of the incident circulated widely on social media and is being used by district investigators.

articles.headline: Oklahoma principal shot in leg is praised for tackling school shooter: 'He is a hero'
articles.image_alt: Pauls Valley High School Principal Kirk Moore
articles.excerpt: Principal Kirk Moore tackled a 20-year-old gunman at Pauls Valley High School on April 7, disarming him with the help of an assistant principal. Moore was shot in the leg during the struggle but held the suspect until police arrived.

articles.headline: Student violence causes SC teachers to quit. Legislators are working on a solution.
articles.excerpt: South Carolina educators are leaving the profession over escalating student violence, with reports of concussions, torn tendons, and one miscarriage caused by student assaults. A House bill to remove repeatedly violent students stalled in committee over equity concerns.

articles.headline: Protecting teachers from workplace violence as student behavior challenges rise
articles.image_alt: Teacher in a classroom representing workplace safety concerns
articles.excerpt: Educator-victimization rates are climbing, and schools are experimenting with safety committees, response protocols and panic-alarm hardware. The piece argues districts must treat assaults on educators as workplace-violence incidents that demand a formal employer response.

articles.headline: Bill advances to protect teachers, but critics warn of impact on students of color and rural parishes
articles.excerpt: Louisiana House Bill 283 — the Teacher Shield Act — cleared the House on April 8 and is now under Senate review. The bill would require immediate removal and alternative placement for students who assault teachers, plus expanded leave for educator recovery.

archive.headline: Painesville Riverside High School student arrested after being found with gun in school, officials say
archive.image_alt: Riverside High School in Painesville, Ohio
archive.excerpt: A Riverside High School student was arrested April 24 after administrators received a tip about a threatening image posted to social media from inside the school. Lake County deputies found the student in a classroom with a firearm. The lockdown was lifted within 22 minutes; no one was injured.

archive.headline: Swansea parents arraigned after second-grader allegedly brought gun to school
archive.excerpt: Christopher and Heather Spangler of Swansea were each arraigned Wednesday on 13 firearm and child-endangerment charges after police say their second-grader brought a handgun to Hoyle Elementary School in his backpack. Both pleaded not guilty and were ordered to surrender all firearms.

archive.headline: AI phone call prompts lockdown at South Sioux City High School, police say
archive.image_alt: South Sioux City High School exterior
archive.excerpt: An AI-generated phone call claiming a pipe bomb in the building and a shotgun on campus triggered a lockdown at South Sioux City High School on April 22. Police, sheriff's deputies, and Nebraska State Patrol cleared the school within an hour. No threat was found.

archive.headline: Student arrested at Righetti High School with loaded handgun, second such arrest this school year
archive.image_alt: Righetti High School campus in Santa Maria
archive.excerpt: An 18-year-old student was arrested at Righetti High School in Santa Maria on April 22 after a school resource deputy serving an unrelated warrant discovered a loaded handgun in his possession. He was booked on felony possession of a firearm on school grounds.

archive.headline: Grissom High School student arrested after weapons-detection system flags pistol at entrance
archive.image_alt: Grissom High School in Huntsville, Alabama
archive.excerpt: A 16-year-old Grissom High School junior was arrested April 21 after Huntsville's campus weapons-detection system flagged a handgun in his backpack as he tried to enter. The student never made it inside and has been charged with unlawful possession of a pistol by a minor.

archive.headline: Dozens of first responders flood Valley Forge High School after cafeteria shooting
archive.image_alt: Emergency responders at Valley Forge High School
archive.excerpt: A student died after a shooting in the cafeteria of Valley Forge High School in Parma Heights, Ohio on April 20. The incident prompted an immediate lockdown and response from dozens of first responders from across the region.

archive.headline: Teacher charged after hoax triggers lockdown at Splendora High School, sheriff says
archive.excerpt: A Splendora, Texas high school teacher was charged with filing a false report and tampering with evidence after investigators determined that an alleged student assault that triggered a campus lockdown on April 9 was a hoax with self-inflicted injuries.

archive.headline: Capitol Access Minute: Legislation advances, along with Teacher Shield Act
archive.excerpt: Louisiana Public Radio's Capitol Access Minute covers the April 8 advancement of the Teacher Shield Act (HB 283) through the House. Rep. Candace Newell, a former teacher, authored the bill; Sen. Gary Carter will carry it in the Senate.
